sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the transcript:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, presented with a 7/10 neck pain, which started approximately a week ago following a car accident where he was hit in the back and his neck and head jerked forward.
The pain is not constant but worsens with movement of the neck.
He has tried ibuprofen with minimal relief.
The pain is localized to the back of the neck and has not radiated to the upper back or shoulders.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing football years ago without any issues with his neck.
He has not mentioned any other relevant medical conditions or social history.
Review of systems: The patient denies any hearing problems, visual disturbances, or other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, pain with flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending of the neck.
Test Results: The patient's x-ray showed no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has neck strain, specifically whiplash, likely caused by the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; Whiplash; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes, and Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I instructed the patient to avoid straining his neck and to relax it as much as possible.
I also ordered an MRI to rule out any potential nerve impingement.
I referred the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me to discuss the MRI results and further treatment options.
